name: Hamburger Jerky
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Curing salt (optional, contains nitrates and sugar; substitute an equal amount of seasoned salt)
recipeInstructions:
1. Buy the leanest ground beef possible or select a lean chuck roast to grind.
2. Combine meat with remaining ingredients, mixing well.
3. Place seasoned meat on plastic wrap covering a drying tray.
4. Using a moistened rolling pin, roll the meat to 1/8 inch thickness, spreading evenly over the tray.
5. Alternatively, use a jerky press and follow manufacturer's directions.
6. Remove the tray with the plastic wrap side up; discard the wrap.
7. Roll paper towels over the top with the rolling pin to remove any melted fat.
8. Invert the meat onto another tray covered with plastic wrap; discard the first wrap.
9. Return the meat to the dehydrator and dry for another 4 to 6 hours.
10. Top with paper towels, roll again to absorb excess fat, and continue drying until jerky is hard and leathery.
11. Cut into strips before storing.
12. Package airtight and store in the refrigerator or freezer if possible; use within 6 months.
